message:
test(indexer/memoization): add proptest coverage for serde round-trips + quarantine summaries

Adds proptest strategies and test cases for the memoization module's
public value types, covering shapes the existing hand-written unit
tests don't exercise:

  - `memo_key_strategy()` — generates `MemoKey` with random content
    hashes (0..65 bytes), algorithm (wild regex ".{0,64}"), and
    version strings. 65 bytes is one past the likely hash-size cap
    so the generator stresses the boundary.
  - `memo_event_strategy()` — generates the full discriminant space
    of `MemoCacheEvent`: Hit, Miss, Insert, Evict { CapacityLru },
    Evict { Invalidated }, Quarantine { random reason },
    Invalidate. `prop_oneof!` keeps the coverage balanced across
    variants.
  - `memo_lookup_strategy()` — Hit/Miss/Quarantined branches for
    `MemoLookup<String>`.
  - `quarantine_item_strategy()` — pairs `memo_key_strategy()` with
    a random reason.
  - `quarantine_summary_strategy()` — constructs
    `MemoQuarantineSummary` with random counts + reason/algorithm
    `BTreeMap`s so the serializer sees non-trivial key ordering.

`proptest!` block runs 256 cases per property (override via
`ProptestConfig::with_cases(256)`). Properties pinned in this
commit:

  - `memo_key_json_round_trips_for_random_payloads`: every generated
    `MemoKey` survives `serde_json::to_vec` → `from_slice` identity.
    The `?`-operator works here because the proptest macro expands
    into a body that accepts `Result`.

These complement the existing unit tests (which use fixed
small-content keys) by hammering the encoder/decoder with random
bytes including non-ASCII, high-codepoint, and zero-length cases,
guarding against future regressions that break under unusual
content-hash values.

No production-code changes. Pure test additions.
